images.caption: Figure 2:Our layout generation pipelineversusexisting methods that treat layouts as language or rely on vision-based optimization.

Figure 3:Overview of theLaviGenframework for autoregressive 3D layout generation.(a) LaviGen formulates layout generation as an autoregressive process. Specifically, conditioned on LLM-encoded instructions, it takes the current scene stateSiS_{i}and objectOiO_{i}to generate the updated stateSi+1S_{i+1}. (b) The high-fidelity scene is obtained by computing the spatial difference betweenSi+1S_{i+1}andSiS_{i}to locate the newly generated region, and fitting the objectOiO_{i}to derive its spatial parameters.

Figure 4:The overview of theadapted 3D diffusion model.
The encoded scene state and object are concatenated with the noisy latent and, together with the identity-aware embedding, fed into the multimodal diffusion transformer for noise prediction. The denoised output is then decoded to produce the updated scene state.

Figure 5:Qualitative comparison of text-to-3D-layout generation.LaviGenproduces physically plausible and spatially coherent layouts aligned with text prompts, effectively avoiding common failure cases such as object collisions (e.g., “gaming room”) and floating artifacts (e.g., “children’s room”, “deli”) observed in baselines.

Figure 6:Qualitative results for layout editing.LaviGen’sunified framework supports context-aware modifications, including object insertion (top row) and removal (bottom row). By operating directly in 3D space, the model performs edits that are spatially coherent and semantically consistent with the surrounding context, and enables direct manipulation of 3D layouts, where previous methods struggle.

Figure 7:Qualitative ablation study forLaviGen.We show the progressive improvement from the base model (left) to the full model (right).
The baseline produces cluttered layouts with severe collisions, while adding the identity-aware embedding yields a more plausible distribution but still suffers collisions from exposure bias. Distillation with holistic guidance yields inaccurate object fitting and severe inversion errors for small objects. In contrast, the full model generates physically plausible and semantically coherent layouts.
Figure 8:Qualitative results for long-sequence generation with more than 20 objects.
Figure 10:Diverse layout results generated from the same input instruction.
